milestones.label: Appraisal inspection completed
milestones.desc: The appraiser visited the property with utilities on. Your lender is working in parallel while the court process runs.

milestones.label: Court noticing period ended
milestones.desc: The 21-day noticing window ran out with no objection stopping the sale. The file is now with the court for the judge's sign-off.

milestones.label: Judge has signed — awaiting written notice delivery
milestones.desc: The judge signed the court approval document (7/21). Audry (listing agent) is waiting for the copy from the attorney's office to forward to Placer Title. Every deadline in this deal begins the first day after written notice is delivered to the buyer — that day is coming very soon.

milestones.label: Contingency windows open
milestones.desc: Standard contingency periods run from the approval date, followed by your final walk-through and closing.

milestones.label: Close of Escrow (tentative)
milestones.desc: The real closing date is set once the court approves. Deed records and the property is yours.

agentNote.author: james
agentNote.body: David and Stacy, great news — the judge has signed the court approval document. We are waiting for the attorney's office to deliver a copy to escrow, and the moment written notice is delivered, every deadline officially begins and we will move quickly to close. James will be in touch with your closing timeline as soon as we have the document in hand. Please make sure your lender has everything they need — we want to be ready to move fast.

clientTasks.label: Sit tight on the court process — nothing is required from you while the judge's sign-off is pending
Keep your loan file current with your lender so financing stays ready the moment the court approves

goodToKnow.title: Why the wait?
goodToKnow.body: This is a court-supervised sale. By contract, all time periods start only after the seller delivers written notice of court approval. The noticing period is done; the judge's sign-off is the last step.

goodToKnow.title: Your deposit is protected
goodToKnow.body: If the court declines to approve the sale, your earnest money deposit is returned to you.

goodToKnow.title: You're ahead of the game
goodToKnow.body: The septic inspection (clean), the appraisal inspection, and your loan paperwork are already done or moving, so the remaining steps can move quickly once approval lands.

deals.notes: UNDER CONTRACT / ACCEPTED 6/3/2026 — BANKRUPTCY COURT SALE (holding pattern). Buyer-side, Legacy Properties (James Beyersdorf's deal; buyer's agent of record on RPA is Sara Cooper — both on transaction via AAA). Buyers David Allen Hopkins & Stacy Ann Gebhart; seller Marshall Graham Guthrie (listing Audry Burton / Brandi Jenkins, Busy Bee Realty). MLS 226049846, Green Hills Sub. $375,000; SCO1 signed 6/2, buyers accepted 6/3; seller pays 2.5% buyer-broker at COE. Financing: FHA (FHA-vs-203K being confirmed with appraiser/lender), loan $361,875 (96.5%), down $1,875, deposit $11,250 (wired, EMD receipt on file). CONTINGENCY CLOCK NOT STARTED: per ADM1 (Bankruptcy Court Approval Contingency), ALL time periods — inspections, contingencies, removal periods, Close of Escrow, all performance — commence the first day AFTER seller delivers written notice of Bankruptcy Court approval to buyer. Status (per listing agent 6/19): motion to sell filed ~6/18; 21-day noticing period running; an objection could restart it; trustee has not confirmed post-approval-to-close timeline. Escrow opened a PLACEHOLDER COE of 7/20 (based on acceptance) but real COE resets to court approval. Buyers proceeding voluntarily to keep things moving: SEPTIC inspection DONE — came back CLEAN; appraisal ordered (appraiser proposed inspection Wed 6/24 10 AM; UTILITIES must be ON for it — needs resolving). If the court/bank declines, buyers get their deposit back. Lender Kim Hesser (Prime Residential) working in parallel so closing may not slip once approved.

Confirm appraisal type: ordered FHA, may need FHA 203K

deals.notes: Buyer-side. OFFER ACCEPTED (confirmed by Sara 6/22/2026). Offer (CA RPA) signed via Authentisign and sent to listing agents Kylie & Mitzi Windley (Chase International) 6/14/2026. FINANCING CHANGED AGAIN (per Sara 6/29): buyers had switched from 20% down to the GSFA 'Assist to Own' grant + family gift, BUT the buyer GOT A RAISE and NO LONGER QUALIFIES for GSFA — financing must be re-worked (likely back to conventional); RPA terms to be updated to reflect whatever the new financing is. agentNote.body: Ashley, congratulations, your offer is accepted and escrow is open with Orange Coast Title. Your earnest money is in. I am confirming a few final details on price and next steps and will walk you through each one.

deals.notes: Pivot for Ashley Robinson (James's buyer) after the 992 Iroquois offer went dead 7/12. Offer sent to listing agent Dee 7/12 for 695 Feather Dr, Copperopolis. Docs in _Offers/Buy Side/Robinson 695 Feather Dr/. UPDATE 7/18 (from James's 7/18 Gmail review, cross-checked against Sara's Gmail): Offer accepted 7/15; escrow opened at Orange Coast Title Company of Southern California (Santa Ana), Escrow #2473532-TS, escrow officer Tanya Salgado (tanyas@octitle.com) — switched from Placer Title because the property was acquired via tax sale last year and Placer is restricted from insuring/escrowing such properties for 3-5 years. EMD confirmed received by escrow 7/16. ClosingLock wire instructions sent to both buyers 7/15-7/16; Ashley Robinson submitted proof of funds 7/16. NOT yet confirmed: final accepted purchase price/terms (a preliminary net sheet referenced a $170,000 cash-purchase figure sent 7/15 before escrow opened — not verified as final); what listing agent Dee Bradley committed to doing "Monday" (7/20) per her 7/17 email; and what the "Page 17" document escrow sent 7/17 actually is (likely the RPA escrow acknowledgment James requested, not confirmed).
